levels.body: A dirge is a type of song that developed as part of the Latin rite of the Office of the Dead, which sometimes included the Evensong or the Mass ("dirge, *n*." 1.). Because of its origins, a dirge commonly refers to "a song of mourning or lament" ("dirge, *n*." 2.). It is sung during burials, funerals, or memorial rites to commemorate the dead, and frequently exhibits characteristics of a funeral march ("dirge").
worksCited: "dirge, *n*." *OED Online*, Oxford University Press, September 2023, 	https://doi.org/10.1093/OED/2928870850. Accessed 27 November 2023.
"dirge." *The Concise Oxford Dictionary of Music*. Edited by Michael Kennedy, and Joyce Bourne Kennedy, 5th ed., Oxford University Press, 2007.
